{"body":"Hello,

For the 'developmental process' branch, we propose to move 'cellular developmental process' under 'cellular process' and rename 'developmental process' -> 'multi-cellular organism developmental process' 

@thomaspd 

Thanks, Pascale","comments":[{"id":"IC_kwDOAkAHws5dwOsb","author":{"login":"raymond91125"},"authorAssociation":"MEMBER","body":"Proposed changes:

- [ ] CREATE NEW TERM 'multicellular organismal-level developmental process' under which to move organism-level processes (embryonic development?? see https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Animal_embryonic_development)

- [ ] 'GO:0048532 anatomical structure arrangement' > move under new term 'multicellular organismal-level developmental process'

@thomaspd

- [ ] CREATE NEW TERM 'multicellular organismal-level developmental process' under which to move organism-level processes (embryonic development?? see https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Animal_embryonic_development) 

Review children of developmental process: 
- [ ] 'GO:0048532 anatomical structure arrangement' > move under new term 'multicellular organismal-level developmental process'

- [ ] GO:0031128 developmental induction' > move under new term 'multicellular organismal-level developmental process'
 - [ ] GO:0021700 developmental maturation' > move under new term 'multicellular organismal-level developmental process'
- [ ] GO:0048757 'pigment granule maturation'  >  not a developmental process (remove is_a developmental maturation')

- [ ] GO:0022611 dormancy process: is a developmental process?

*— @dragon-ai-agent*","createdAt":"2026-05-18T22:49:05Z","includesCreatedEdit":false,"isMinimized":false,"minimizedReason":"","reactionGroups":[],"url":"https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/issues/22994#issuecomment-4482924123","viewerDidAuthor":true},{"id":"IC_kwDOAkAHws8AAAABCzR8sg","author":{"login":"dragon-ai-agent"},"authorAssociation":"MEMBER","body":"@raymond91125 Analysis of developmental process terms that should be `is_a` children of **GO:0160108** (animal gross anatomical part developmental process) or **GO:0160109** (plant gross anatomical part developmental process).

## Method

Both grouping terms describe *"a developmental process whose specific outcome is the progression of a **gross anatomical part** of an animal/plant from an initial immature state to a later, more mature state"*. In GO this is exactly the `anatomical structure development` (GO:0048856) pattern, whose subclasses carry the logical definition:

```
intersection_of: GO:0048856 ! anatomical structure development
intersection_of: results_in_development_of <ANATOMY TERM>
```

I enumerated every non-obsolete term carrying `results_in_development_of` and partitioned by the differentia's source ontology, applying the principle you stated in this thread — *"a cell is not a gross anatomical part"* — to exclude cell-level and subcellular terms.

## Results

| Bucket | Differentia | Count | Disposition |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Animal** | UBERON (multicellular anatomical structure) | **590** | → `is_a` GO:0160108 |
| **Plant** | PO (plant anatomical structure, excl. plant cells) | **61** | → `is_a` GO:0160109 |
| Animal cell development | CL | 99 | *not* gross anatomical part → cellular developmental process |
| Plant cell development | PO (4 plant-cell terms) | 4 | *not* gross anatomical part → cellular developmental process |
| Subcellular | GO CC (dendrite, pollen tube, apical/basal dendrite) | 4 | cellular-component level — out of scope |
| Fungal | FAO (fruitbody, conidium, ascus, basidium, …) | 10 | neither animal nor plant — needs separate grouping |
| Dictyostelium | DDANAT (GO:0048837 sorocarp sorus development) | 1 | neither animal nor plant (cf. @pfey03 discussion) |

`GO:0048856 anatomical structure development` itself (differentia = UBERON:0000061 *anatomical structure*, the root) is the **genus / superclass**, not a child, and is excluded from the 590.

The 4 plant-cell terms excluded from GO:0160109: GO:0010441 guard cell development, GO:0080147 root hair cell development, GO:0090393 sepal giant cell development, GO:0090436 leaf pavement cell development.

## Recommended implementation (no ontology edits made)

This is a "parent relationship query", so I have **not** modified the ontology — asserting ~650 `is_a` edges by hand is unmaintainable and the approach still has open points in this thread. The GO-idiomatic fix is to give the two grouping terms logical definitions and let the reasoner classify all 651 automatically:

```
id: GO:0160108
intersection_of: GO:0048856 ! anatomical structure development
intersection_of: results_in_development_of UBERON:0010000 ! multicellular anatomical structure

id: GO:0160109
intersection_of: GO:0048856 ! anatomical s

content.content:
TITLE: Changes to 'developmental process' 

BODY:
Hello, 

 For the BP refactoring, we want to untangle multi-cellular organism processes from cellular processes. 
For the 'developmental process' branch, we propose to move 'cellular developmental process' under 'cellular process' and rename 'developmental process' -> 'multi-cellular organism developmental process' 

@thomaspd 

Thanks, Pascale

--- COMMENTS ---

=== Comment 1 by raymond91125 (id=IC_kwDOAkAHws5dwOsb) ===
Proposed changes: 

- [ ] move 'GO:0048869 cellular developmental process' to 'is_a 'GO:0009987 cellular process'

- [ ] CREATE NEW TERM 'multicellular organismal-level developmental process' under which to move organism-level processes (embryonic development?? see https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Animal_embryonic_development) 

Review children of developmental process: 
- [ ] 'GO:0048532 anatomical structure arrangement' > move under new term 'multicellular organismal-level developmental process'
- [ ] 'GO:0048856 anatomical structure development' > move under new term 'multicellular organismal-level developmental process'
- [ ] 'GO:0048646 anatomical structure formation involved in morphogenesis' > move under new term 'multicellular organismal-level developmental process'
- [ ] 'GO:0009653 anatomical structure morphogenesis' > move under new term 'multicellular organismal-level developmental process'
- [ ] 'GO:0060033 anatomical structure regression' > move under new term 'multicellular organismal-level developmental process'
- [ ] GO:0043697 cell dedifferentiation > move under GO:0048869 cellular developmental process
- [ ] GO:0043696 dedifferentiation > obsolete, unnecessary grouping class
- [ ] GO:0031128 developmental induction' > move under new term 'multicellular organismal-level developmental process'
 - [ ] GO:0021700 developmental maturation' > move under new term 'multicellular organismal-level developmental process'
- [ ] GO:0048757 'pigment granule maturation'  >  not a developmental process (remove is_a developmental maturation')
- [ ] GO:0061792 secretory granule maturation > not a developmental process/ is_a  GO:0033363 secretory granule organization sufficient (remove LD)
- [ ] GO:0016188 synaptic vesicle maturation  > not a developmental process/ is_a   'vesicle organization' sufficient (remove LD)
- [ ] GO:0060074 'synapse maturation'  not a developmental process is_ GO:0050808 synapse organization sufficient (remove is_a developm

=== Comment 2 by raymond91125 (id=IC_kwDOAkAHws5dwPRr) ===
@pgaudet  
CREATE NEW TERM 'multicellular organismal-level developmental process' under which to move organism-level processes (embryonic development?? see https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Animal_embryonic_development)
IS_A developmental process?
If we are to keep the grouping term developmental process, shouldn't cellular developmental process stay? cellular developmental process has dual IS_A parentage.

---unicellular organism-level (or merge with cellular)
---cellular component-level

Discussion with @thomaspd and @raymond91125 
- cellular component-level should go under 'organelle biogenesis' (or something like this, depending on the term 
- for now we can keep the top level term ' developmental process', and decide later when looking more broadly at the ontology
- specifically: under ' developmental process', we can keep for now: 

 - cellular developmental process
-  ~multicellular organism-level~
-  NTR animal developmental process
-  NTR  plant developmental process
-   unicellular organism-level (or merge with cellular) (maybe we create later, when we see whether we need it?)
-  cellular process
- -  GO:0032989 cellular component morphogenesis

=== Comment 5 by raymond91125 (id=IC_kwDOAkAHws5eXFhk) ===
New term proposal
animal organism-level developmental process "Any multicellular organismal process whose specific outcome is the progression of a metazoa over time from an initial condition to a later condition." [WIKIPEDIA:Developmental biology]
plant organism-level developmental process "Any multicellular organismal process whose specific outcome is the progression of a  viridiplantae over time from an initial condition to a later condition." [WIKIPEDIA:Developmental biology]


=== Comment 6 by pgaudet (id=IC_kwDOAkAHws5ebE-h) ===
Sound good. Could we simplify the labels: 
'animal developmental process' and 'plant developmental process' ? 

Thanks, Pascale

=== Comment 7 by raymond91125 (id=IC_kwDOAkAHws5efZZu) ===
I just noticed that there is already a term multicellular organism development with LD 'anatomical structure development'
 and ('results in developmental progression of' some 'multicellular organism') and two other IS_A parents developmental process, multicellular organismal process.

=== Comment 8 by raymond91125 (id=IC_kwDOAkAHws5efcD3) ===
I think the animal and plant terms can be placed under multicellular organism development and revise the definition accordingly. One concern is that there maybe developmental processes that are beyond clearly identifiable anatomical structures, e.g. gender identity, gender euphoria. GO currently does not cover these psychological processes.

LD for plant term: need to coordinate with PO to create a suitable grouping class

Dicty is neither plant nor animal, though because it's from the base in phylogeny where plants and animals divide, it has many (about 30% mammalian ortholgs but no plant orthologs, and of cause general orthologs from  yeast to up, and then purely amoeba genes. @raymond91125 Analysis of developmental process terms that should be `is_a` children of **GO:0160108** (animal gross anatomical part developmental process) or **GO:0160109** (plant gross anatomical part developmental process).

## Method

Both grouping terms describe *"a developmental process whose specific outcome is the progression of a **gross anatomical part** of an animal/plant from an initial immature state to a later, more mature state"*. In GO this is exactly the `anatomical structure development` (GO:0048856) pattern, whose subclasses carry the logical definition:

```
intersection_of: GO:0048856 ! anatomical structure development
intersection_of: results_in_development_of <ANATOMY TERM>
```

I enumerated every non-obsolete term carrying `results_in_development_of` and partitioned by the differentia's source ontology, applying the principle you stated in this thread — *"a cell is not a gross anatomical part"* — to exclude cell-level and subcellular terms.

## Results

| Bucket | Differentia | Count | Disposition |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Animal** | UBERON (multicellular anatomical structure) | **590** | → `is_a` GO:0160108 |
| **Plant** | PO (plant anatomical structure, excl. plant cells) | **61** | → `is_a` GO:0160109 |
| Animal cell development | CL | 99 | *not* gross anatomical part → cellular developmental process |
| Plant cell development | PO (4 plant-cell terms) | 4 | *not* gross anatomical part → cellular developmental process |
| Subcellular | GO CC (dendrite, pollen tube, apical/basal dendrite) | 4 | cellular-component level — out of scope |
| Fungal | FAO (fruitbody, conidium, ascus, basidium, …) | 10 | neither animal nor plant — needs separate grouping |
| Dictyostelium | DDANAT (GO:0048837 sorocarp sorus development) | 1 | neither animal nor plant (cf. @pfey03 discussion) |

`GO:0048856 anatomical structure development` itself (differentia = UBERON:0000061 *anatomical structure*, the root) is the **genus / superclass**, not a child, and is exclu

=== Comment 38 by raymond91125 (id=IC_kwDOAkAHws8AAAABCzw_Ow) ===
https://github.com/Planteome/plant-ontology/issues/718

=== Comment 39 by raymond91125 (id=IC_kwDOAkAHws8AAAABCzxlpA) ===
@dragon-ai-agent please I've updated uberon import to include UBERON:0010000. Add logical definition for GO:0160108.

=== Comment 40 by dragon-ai-agent (id=IC_kwDOAkAHws8AAAABCzxq1w) ===
🤖 Working on it...

Follow along: [View workflow run](https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/actions/runs/26069085339)

*— @dragon-ai-agent*

=== Comment 41 by dragon-ai-agent (id=IC_kwDOAkAHws8AAAABCz3Mrw) ===
@raymond91125 Done. `UBERON:0010000` is now in the uberon import, so I added the logical definition to **GO:0160108** (*animal gross anatomical part developmental process*) and opened #32123.

## What changed

GO:0160108 now has a genus-differentia logical definition following the standard *anatomical structure development* design pattern:

> *animal gross anatomical part developmental process* ≡ **anatomical structure development** (GO:0048856) that **results in development of** some **multicellular anatomical structure** (UBERON:0010000)

`UBERON:0010000` is "an anatomical structure that has more than one cell as a part" — i.e. exactly a *gross anatomical part*. Because a single cell is not a multicellular structure, this automatically does the right thing with respect to the principle raised earlier in this thread: **cells are not gross anatomical parts**, so cell-level terms stay out, and plant (PO) terms stay out too (those belong under the sibling GO:0160109).

The asserted parent was tightened from the very general *developmental process* (GO:0032502) to the genus *anatomical structure development* (GO:0048856), consistent with existing terms like *blood vessel development*.

## Effect

The reasoner now classifies **555** developmental-process terms under GO:0160108 — no hand-asserted `is_a` edges needed. Spot-checked classification:

- ✅ Animal terms placed correctly: blood vessel, eye, metanephric glomerulus, larynx development
- ✅ Plant terms correctly excluded: flower development, root development
- ✅ Cell terms correctly excluded: glial cell development, T cell differentiation

## Validation

`make travis_build` passes (all 20 SPARQL QC rules, 0 violations; ELK reasoning consistent, no unsatisfiable classes). Pre-change reasoning baseline also confirmed valid.

## Still open (not in this PR)

GO:0160109 (the plant counterpart) still needs the agreed PO grouping class — see Planteome/plant-ontology#718 — before it can get an analogous logical definition.


=== Comment 42 by raymond91125 (id=IC_kwDOAkAHws8AAAABC9EaCA) ===

=== Comment 37 by dragon-ai-agent (id=IC_kwDOAkAHws8AAAABCzR8sg) ===
@raymond91125 Analysis of developmental process terms that should be `is_a` children of **GO:0160108** (animal gross anatomical part developmental process) or **GO:0160109** (plant gross anatomical part developmental process).

## Method

Both grouping terms describe *"a developmental process whose specific outcome is the progression of a **gross anatomical part** of an animal/plant from an initial immature state to a later, more mature state"*. In GO this is exactly the `anatomical structure development` (GO:0048856) pattern, whose subclasses carry the logical definition:

```
intersection_of: GO:0048856 ! anatomical structure development
intersection_of: results_in_development_of <ANATOMY TERM>
```

I enumerated every non-obsolete term carrying `results_in_development_of` and partitioned by the differentia's source ontology, applying the principle you stated in this thread — *"a cell is not a gross anatomical part"* — to exclude cell-level and subcellular terms.

## Results

| Bucket | Differentia | Count | Disposition |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Animal** | UBERON (multicellular anatomical structure) | **590** | → `is_a` GO:0160108 |
| **Plant** | PO (plant anatomical structure, excl. plant cells) | **61** | → `is_a` GO:0160109 |
| Animal cell development | CL | 99 | *not* gross anatomical part → cellular developmental process |
| Plant cell development | PO (4 plant-cell terms) | 4 | *not* gross anatomical part → cellular developmental process |
| Subcellular | GO CC (dendrite, pollen tube, apical/basal dendrite) | 4 | cellular-component level — out of scope |
| Fungal | FAO (fruitbody, conidium, ascus, basidium, …) | 10 | neither animal nor plant — needs separate grouping |
| Dictyostelium | DDANAT (GO:0048837 sorocarp sorus development) | 1 | neither animal nor plant (cf. @pfey03 discussion) |

`GO:0048856 anatomical structure development` itself (differentia = UBERON:0000061 *anatomical structure*, the root) is the **genus / superclass**, not a child, and is exclu

=== Comment 40 by dragon-ai-agent (id=IC_kwDOAkAHws8AAAABCzxq1w) ===

=== Comment 41 by dragon-ai-agent (id=IC_kwDOAkAHws8AAAABCz3Mrw) ===
@raymond91125 Done. `UBERON:0010000` is now in the uberon import, so I added the logical definition to **GO:0160108** (*animal gross anatomical part developmental process*) and opened #32123.

## What changed

GO:0160108 now has a genus-differentia logical definition following the standard *anatomical structure development* design pattern:

> *animal gross anatomical part developmental process* ≡ **anatomical structure development** (GO:0048856) that **results in development of** some **multicellular anatomical structure** (UBERON:0010000)

`UBERON:0010000` is "an anatomical structure that has more than one cell as a part" — i.e. exactly a *gross anatomical part*. Because a single cell is not a multicellular structure, this automatically does the right thing with respect to the principle raised earlier in this thread: **cells are not gross anatomical parts**, so cell-level terms stay out, and plant (PO) terms stay out too (those belong under the sibling GO:0160109).

The asserted parent was tightened from the very general *developmental process* (GO:0032502) to the genus *anatomical structure development* (GO:0048856), consistent with existing terms like *blood vessel development*.

## Effect

The reasoner now classifies **555** developmental-process terms under GO:0160108 — no hand-asserted `is_a` edges needed. Spot-checked classification:

- ✅ Animal terms placed correctly: blood vessel, eye, metanephric glomerulus, larynx development
- ✅ Plant terms correctly excluded: flower development, root development
- ✅ Cell terms correctly excluded: glial cell development, T cell differentiation

## Validation

`make travis_build` passes (all 20 SPARQL QC rules, 0 violations; ELK reasoning consistent, no unsatisfiable classes). Pre-change reasoning baseline also confirmed valid.

## Still open (not in this PR)

GO:0160109 (the plant counterpart) still needs the agreed PO grouping class — see Planteome/plant-ontology#718 — before it can get an analogous logical definition.

{"body":"## Summary

Closes the open action on #22994: now that `UBERON:0010000` (*multicellular anatomical structure*) is in the uberon import, GO:0160108 gets a genus-differentia logical definition so the reasoner can classify the animal developmental-process terms automatically instead of asserting hundreds of `is_a` edges by hand.

```obo
[Term]
id: GO:0160108
name: animal gross anatomical part developmental process
namespace: biological_process
def: "Any developmental process whose specific outcome is the progression of a gross anatomical part of an animal from an initial immature state, to a later, more mature state." [GOC:pg]
synonym: "animal development" RELATED []
is_a: GO:0048856 ! anatomical structure development
intersection_of: GO:0048856 ! anatomical structure development
intersection_of: results_in_development_of UBERON:0010000 ! multicellular anatomical structure
property_value: term_tracker_item "https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/issues/22994" xsd:anyURI
```

### Rationale

- Conforms exactly to the **anatomical structure development** design pattern (`src/design_patterns/anatomical_structure_development.yaml`): `'anatomical structure development' and 'results in development of' some <target ⊑ UBERON:0000061>`.
- `UBERON:0010000` = "an anatomical structure that has more than one cell as a part" — this is precisely a *gross anatomical part* (more than one cell). It implements the thread principle that **a cell is not a gross anatomical part**: single cells (CL) are not subclasses of UBERON:0010000, so cell-level development terms are correctly excluded; PO plant terms are likewise excluded (they belong under the sibling GO:0160109).
- Asserted `is_a` changed from `GO:0032502` (developmental process) → genus `GO:0048856` (anatomical structure development) to avoid an over-general asserted parent now that an equivalence axiom is present (matches prior art, e.g. GO:0001568 blood vessel development).
- No metadata (`created_by`/`creation_date`) touched — this is an existing term.

### Validation

- [x] PLAN — issue analyzed; intent unambiguous (explicit request + prior analysis in thread)
- [x] PRE-VALIDATION — ELK reasoning of unmodified `go-edit.obo` passed (exit 0)
- [x] TERM-SEARCH — confirmed `UBERON:0010000` present in `imports/uberon_import.obo`; GO:0048856 / `results_in_development_of` usage confirmed
- [x] DESIGN-PATTERNS — `anatomical_structure_development.yaml` consulted; prior art (blood vessel, eye, extraocular muscle development) reviewed
- [x] EDITS — checkout/checkin procedure via `obo-checkout.pl`/`obo-checkin.pl`
- [x] RELATIONSHIPS — single genus `is_a` = logical-def genus; equivalence axiom (necessary AND sufficient), not over-specified; mirrors text def & label
- [x] METADATA — unchanged (existing term); `term_tracker_item` already points to #22994
- [x] AUTOMATED-VALIDATION — `make travis_build` passes (exit 0); all 20 SPARQL QC rules: 0 violations; ELK reasoning consistent, no unsatisfiable classes
- [x] CLASSIFICATION CHECK — reasoned `go-edit` merged with uberon/cl/po imports: **555** GO developmental-process terms classify under GO:0160108. Spot checks: blood vessel / eye / metanephric glomerulus / larynx development → UNDER (correct); flower / root development (plant), glial cell development / T cell differentiation (cell) → NOT under (correct)
- [x] REFERENCE-VALIDATION — N/A (no new literature references introduced)

### Reviewer notes

- The descendant count is 555 (vs. the ~590 `results_in_development_of UBERON` count from the earlier thread analysis). The difference is expected: the earlier figure counted raw `results_in_development_of UBERON` axioms; 555 is the count of terms the reasoner actually places under GO:0160108 via the UBERON subclass hierarchy present in the import module. No plant/cell leakage observed.
- The sibling GO:0160109 (plant) still needs the agreed PO grouping class before it can receive an analogous logical definition — out of scope for this PR.

namespace: biological_process
def: "The process whose specific outcome is the progression of the anterior Malpighian tubule over time, from its formation to the mature structure. def: "The process whose specific outcome is the progression of the trigeminal nerve over time, from its formation to the mature structure. The trigeminal nerve is composed of three large branches. They are the ophthalmic (V1, sensory), maxillary (V2, sensory) and mandibular (V3, motor and sensory) branches. The sensory ophthalmic branch travels through the superior orbital fissure and passes through the orbit to reach the skin of the forehead and top of the head. The maxillary nerve contains sensory branches that reach the pterygopalatine fossa via the inferior orbital fissure (face, cheek and upper teeth) and pterygopalatine canal (soft and hard palate, nasal cavity and pharynx). The motor part of the mandibular branch is distributed to the muscles of mastication, the mylohyoid muscle and the anterior belly of the digastric. The mandibular nerve also innervates the tensor veli palatini and tensor tympani muscles. The sensory part of the mandibular nerve is composed of branches that carry general sensory information from the mucous membranes of the mouth and cheek, anterior two-thirds of the tongue, lower teeth, skin of the lower jaw, side of the head and scalp and meninges of the anterior and middle cranial fossae." def: "The process whose specific outcome is the progression of the fourth ventricle over time, from its formation to the mature structure. It is continuous with the central canal of the cord below and with the cerebral aqueduct above, and through its lateral and median apertures it communicates with the subarachnoid space." def: "The process whose specific outcome is the progression of the lung over time, from its formation to the mature structure. In amphibians and many reptiles the lungs retain very nearly this primitive sac-like character, but in the higher forms the connection with the esophagus becomes elongated into the windpipe and the inner walls of the sacs become more and more divided, until, in the mammals, the air spaces become minutely divided into tubes ending in small air cells, in the walls of which the blood circulates in a fine network of capillaries. def: "The formation of a thickening of the neural ectoderm in the head region of the vertebrate embryo which develops into the olfactory region of the nasal cavity." [GOC:dgh]

def: "The process in which a relatively unspecialized cell derived from the embryonic head mesoderm acquires the specialized features of a mature hemocyte. def: "The process in which the anatomical structures of the olfactory placode are generated and organized. The olfactory placode is a thickening of the neural ectoderm in the head region of the vertebrate embryo which develops into the olfactory region of the nasal cavity." [GOC:mah]

tool_use_result.stdout:
def: "Increase in size of antral follicles due to cell proliferation and/or growth of the antral cavity." [https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/books/NBK279054/]
def: "The menstrual cycle process that results in the formation of one central cavity separating the oocyte/cumulus complex from mural granulosa and theca cells during the various stages of oogenesis." [GOC:dph, GOC:tb, https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/books/NBK279054/]
def: "An increase in size of a blastocyst due to expansion of the blastocoelic cavity cell shape changes and cell proliferation." [GOC:dph, ISBN:0124020607, ISBN:0198542771]
comment: See also the Anatomical Dictionary for Mouse Development ontology terms 'TS4, blastocoelic cavity ; EMAP:17', 'TS5, blastocoelic cavity ; EMAP:27' and 'TS6, blastocoelic cavity ; EMAP:36'.
def: "The process in which the anatomical structures of epithelia are generated and organized. An epithelium consists of closely packed cells arranged in one or more layers, that covers the outer surfaces of the body or lines any internal cavity or tube." [GOC:dph, GOC:jl, GOC:tb, ISBN:0198506732]
def: "OBSOLETE. The ovulation cycle process in which one central cavity separating the oocyte/cumulus complex from mural granulosa and theca cells is formed as part of the fused antrum stage of oogenesis." [GOC:dph, GOC:isa_complete]
def: "OBSOLETE. The menstrual cycle process in which one central cavity separating the oocyte/cumulus complex from mural granulosa and theca cells is formed as part of the antral spaces stage of oogenesis." [GOC:dph, GOC:isa_complete]
def: "OBSOLETE. The menstrual cycle process in which one central cavity separating the oocyte/cumulus complex from mural granulosa and theca cells is formed as part of the scattered antral spaces stage of oogenesis." [GOC:dph, GOC:isa_complete]
def: "The progression of a cardiac chamber over time, from its formation to the mature structure. A cardiac chamber is an enclosed cavity within the heart." [GOC:mtg_heart]
def: "The process in which a cardiac chamber is generated and organized. A cardiac chamber is an enclosed cavity within the heart." [GOC:mtg_heart]
def: "The developmental process pertaining to the initial formation of a cardiac chamber from unspecified parts. A cardiac chamber is an enclosed cavity within the heart." [GOC:mtg_heart]
def: "The process whose specific outcome is the progression of the salivary gland over time, from its formation to the mature structure. Salivary glands include any of the saliva-secreting exocrine glands of the oral cavity." [GOC:jid, UBERON:0001044]
def: "The process whose specific outcome is the progression of the hemocyte over time, from its formation to the mature structure. Hemocytes are blood cells associated with a hemocoel (the cavity containing most of the major organs of the arthropod body) which are involved in defense and clotting of hemolymph, but not involved in transport of oxygen." [GOC:bf, GOC:mtg_sensu]
def: "The cavity enclosed within the chloroplast thylakoid membrane. An example of this component is found in Arabidopsis thaliana." [GOC:mtg_sensu, ISBN:0943088399]
def: "A plasmodesma with a branched structure, often with many channels leading into a larger central cavity; found in older tissues and usually derived from preexisting primary plasmodesmata." [PMID:15012255]
def: "The process whose specific outcome is the progression of the olfactory nerve over time, from its formation to the mature structure. The olfactory nerve is a collection of sensory nerve rootlets that extend down from the olfactory bulb to the olfactory mucosa of the upper parts of the nasal cavity. This nerve conducts odor information to the brainstem." [GO_REF:0000021, GOC:cls, GOC:dgh, GOC:dph, GOC:jid, ISBN:0838580343]
def: "The process whose specific outcome is the progression of the trigeminal nerve over time, from its formation to the mature structure. The trigeminal nerve is composed of three large branches. They are the ophthalmic (V1, sensory), maxillary (V2, sensory) and mandibular (V3, motor and sensory) branches. The sensory ophthalmic branch travels through the superior orbital fissure and passes through the orbit to reach the skin of the forehead and top of the head. The maxillary nerve contains sensory branches that reach the pterygopalatine fossa via the inferior orbital fissure (face, cheek and upper teeth) and pterygopalatine canal (soft and hard palate, nasal cavity and pharynx). The motor part of the mandibular branch is distributed to the muscles of mastication, the mylohyoid muscle and the anterior belly of the digastric. The mandibular nerve also innervates the tensor veli palatini and tensor tympani muscles. The sensory part of the mandibular nerve is composed of branches that carry general sensory information from the mucous membranes of the mouth and cheek, anterior two-thirds of the tongue, lower teeth, skin of the lower jaw, side of the head and scalp and meninges of the anterior and middle cranial fossae." [GO_REF:0000021, GOC:cls, GOC:dgh, GOC:dph, GOC:jid, ISBN:0838580343]
def: "The process whose specific outcome is the progression of the fourth ventricle over time, from its formation to the mature structure. The fourth ventricle is an irregularly shaped cavity in the rhombencephalon, between the medulla oblongata, the pons, and the isthmus in front, and the cerebellum behind. It is continuous with the central canal of the cord below and with the cerebral aqueduct above, and through its lateral and median apertures it communicates with the subarachnoid space." [GO_REF:0000021, GOC:cls, GOC:dgh, GOC:dph, GOC:jid, ISBN:0838580343]
def: "The process in which the anatomical structure of the olfactory nerve is generated and organized. def: "The process that gives rise to the olfactory nerve. This process pertains to the initial formation of a structure from unspecified parts. This nerve conducts odor information to the brainstem." [GO_REF:0000021, GOC:cls, GOC:dgh, GOC:dph, GOC:jid, ISBN:0838580343]
def: "The process that contributes to the act of creating the structural organization of the oculomotor nerve. This process pertains to the physical shaping of a rudimentary structure. This nerve conducts odor information to the brainstem." [GO_REF:0000021, GOC:cls, GOC:dgh, GOC:dph, GOC:jid, ISBN:0838580343]
def: "A developmental process, independent of morphogenetic (shape) change, that is required for the olfactory nerve to attain its fully functional state. This nerve conducts odor information to the brainstem." [GO_REF:0000021, GOC:cls, GOC:dgh, GOC:dph, GOC:jid]
def: "A developmental process, independent of morphogenetic (shape) change, that is required for the trigeminal nerve to attain its fully functional state. [GO_REF:0000021, GOC:cls, GOC:dgh, GOC:dph, GOC:jid, ISBN:0838580343]
def: "The process in which the anatomical structure of the trigeminal nerve is generated and organized. The trigeminal nerve is composed of three large branches. They are the ophthalmic (V1, sensory), maxillary (V2, sensory) and mandibular (V3, motor and sensory) branches. The sensory ophthalmic branch travels through the superior orbital fissure and passes through the orbit to reach the skin of the forehead and top of the head. The maxillary nerve contains sensory branches that reach the pterygopalatine fossa via the inferior orbital fissure (face, cheek and upper teeth) and pterygopalatine canal (soft and hard palate, nasal cavity and pharynx). The motor part of the mandibular branch is distributed to the muscles of mastication, the mylohyoid muscle and the anterior belly of the digastric. The mandibular nerve also innervates the tensor veli palatini and tensor tympani muscles. The sensory part of the mandibular nerve is composed of branches that carry general sensory information from the mucous membranes of the mouth and cheek, anterior two-thirds of the tongue, lower teeth, skin of the lower jaw, side of the head and scalp and meninges of the anterior and middle cranial fossae." [GO_REF:0000021, GOC:cls, GOC:dgh, GOC:dph, GOC:jid, ISBN:0838580343]
def: "The process that contributes to the act of creating the structural organization of the oculomotor nerve. This process pertains to the physical shaping of a rudimentary structure. The trigeminal nerve is composed of three large branches. They are the ophthalmic (V1, sensory), maxillary (V2, sensory) and mandibular (V3, motor and sensory) branches. The sensory ophthalmic branch travels through the superior orbital fissure and passes through the orbit to reach the skin of the forehead and top of the head. The maxillary nerve contains sensory branches that reach the pterygopalatine fossa via the inferior orbital fissure (face, cheek and upper teeth) and pterygopalatine canal (soft and hard palate, nasal cavity and pharynx). The motor part of the mandibular branch is distributed to the muscles of mastication, the mylohyoid muscle and the anterior belly of the digastric. The mandibular nerve also innervates the tensor veli palatini and tensor tympani muscles. The sensory part of the mandibular nerve is composed of branches that carry general sensory information from the mucous membranes of the mouth and cheek, anterior two-thirds of the tongue, lower teeth, skin of the lower jaw, side of the head and scalp and meninges of the anterior and middle cranial fossae." [GO_REF:0000021, GOC:cls, GOC:dgh, GOC:dph, GOC:jid, ISBN:0838580343]
def: "The process that gives rise to the trigeminal nerve. This process pertains to the initial formation of a structure from unspecified parts. The trigeminal nerve is composed of three large branches. They are the ophthalmic (V1, sensory), maxillary (V2, sensory) and mandibular (V3, motor and sensory) branches. The sensory ophthalmic branch travels through the superior orbital fissure and passes through the orbit to reach the skin of the forehead and top of the head. The maxillary nerve contains sensory branches that reach the pterygopalatine fossa via the inferior orbital fissure (face, cheek and upper teeth) and pterygopalatine canal (soft and hard palate, nasal cavity and pharynx). The motor part of the mandibular branch is distributed to the muscles of mastication, the mylohyoid muscle and the anterior belly of the digastric. The mandibular nerve also innervates the tensor veli palatini and tensor tympani muscles. The sensory part of the mandibular nerve is composed of branches that carry general sensory information from the mucous membranes of the mouth and cheek, anterior two-thirds of the tongue, lower teeth, skin of the lower jaw, side of the head and scalp and meninges of the anterior and middle cranial fossae." [GO_REF:0000021, GOC:cls, GOC:dgh, GOC:dph, GOC:jid, ISBN:0838580343]
def: "The process whose specific outcome is the progression of the lateral ventricles over time, from the formation to the mature structure. The two lateral ventricles are a cavity in each of the cerebral hemispheres derived from the cavity of the embryonic neural tube. They are separated from each other by the septum pellucidum, and each communicates with the third ventricle by the foramen of Monro, through which also the choroid plexuses of the lateral ventricles become continuous with that of the third ventricle." [GO_REF:0000021, GOC:cls, GOC:dgh, GOC:dph, GOC:jid, ISBN:0838580343]
def: "The multiplication or reproduction of cells, resulting in the expansion of a cell population in the hindbrain region that is adjacent to the ventricular cavity." [GO_REF:0000021, GOC:cls, GOC:dgh, GOC:dph, GOC:jid, PMID:12593981, PMID:15157725]
def: "The process whose specific outcome is the progression of the lung over time, from its formation to the mature structure. In all air-breathing vertebrates the lungs are developed from the ventral wall of the oesophagus as a pouch which divides into two sacs. In amphibians and many reptiles the lungs retain very nearly this primitive sac-like character, but in the higher forms the connection with the esophagus becomes elongated into the windpipe and the inner walls of the sacs become more and more divided, until, in the mammals, the air spaces become minutely divided into tubes ending in small air cells, in the walls of which the blood circulates in a fine network of capillaries. In mammals the lungs are more or less divided into lobes, and each lung occupies a separate cavity in the thorax." [GOC:jid, UBERON:0002048]
def: "The formation of a thickening of the neural ectoderm in the head region of the vertebrate embryo which develops into the olfactory region of the nasal cavity." [GOC:dgh]
def: "The directed movement of a hemocyte within the embryo. Hemocytes are blood cells associated with a hemocoel (the cavity containing most of the major organs of the arthropod body) which are involved in defense and clotting of hemolymph, but not involved in transport of oxygen. In Drosophila, embryonic hemocytes originate from the head mesoderm as a cluster of cells. The cluster splits into two and one group of cells crosses the amnioserosa. Both populations then spread toward the middle of the embryo and then disperse evenly throughout the embryo." [GOC:bf, GOC:mtg_sensu, PMID:12885551]
def: "The process in which a relatively unspecialized cell derived from the embryonic head mesoderm acquires the specialized features of a mature hemocyte. Hemocytes are blood cells associated with a hemocoel (the cavity containing most of the major organs of the arthropod body) which are involved in defense and clotting of hemolymph, but not involved in transport of oxygen." [GOC:bf, GOC:mtg_sensu, PMID:14602069]
def: "The process in which a relatively unspecialized cell derived from the larval lymph gland acquires the specialized features of a mature hemocyte. The lymph gland consists of three to six bilaterally paired lobes that are attached to the cardioblasts during larval stages, and it degenerates during pupal stages. Hemocytes are blood cells associated with a hemocoel (the cavity containing most of the major organs of the arthropod body) which are involved in defense and clotting of hemolymph, but not involved in transport of oxygen. An example of this process is found in Drosophila melanogaster." [GOC:bf, GOC:mtg_sensu, PMID:14602069]
def: "The multiplication or reproduction of hemocytes, resulting in the expansion of the cell population. Hemocytes are blood cells associated with a hemocoel (the cavity containing most of the major organs of the arthropod body) which are involved in defense and clotting of hemolymph, but not involved in transport of oxygen." [GOC:bf, GOC:mtg_sensu]
def: "Any process that modulates the frequency, rate or extent of hemocyte proliferation. Hemocytes are blood cells associated with a hemocoel (the cavity containing most of the major organs of the arthropod body) which are involved in defense and clotting of hemolymph, but not involved in transport of oxygen. An example of this is found in Drosophila melanogaster." [GOC:bf, GOC:mtg_sensu]
def: "Any process that stops, prevents or reduces the rate or extent of hemocyte proliferation. Hemocytes are blood cells associated with a hemocoel (the cavity containing most of the major organs of the arthropod body) which are involved in defense and clotting of hemolymph, but not involved in transport of oxygen. An example of this is found in Drosophila melanogaster." [GOC:bf, GOC:mtg_sensu]
def: "Any process that activates or increases the rate or extent of hemocyte proliferation. Hemocytes are blood cells associated with a hemocoel (the cavity containing most of the major organs of the arthropod body) that are involved in defense and clotting of hemolymph, but not involved in transport of oxygen. An example of this process is found in Drosophila melanogaster." [GOC:bf, GOC:mtg_sensu]
def: "The process whose specific outcome is the progression of the nephrostome over time, from its formation to the mature structure. The nephrostome is the opening of the pronephros into the body cavity." [GOC:mtg_kidney_jan10, PMID:14686690, PMID:15647339, XAO:0000062]
def: "The process in which a relatively unspecialized cell acquires the characteristics of a mature hemocyte. Hemocytes are blood cells associated with a hemocoel (the cavity containing most of the major organs of the arthropod body) which are involved in defense and clotting of hemolymph, but not involved in transport of oxygen." [CL:0000387, GOC:jl, GOC:mtg_sensu, PMID:9550723]
def: "The process in which the anatomical structures of the inner ear are generated and organized. The inner ear is the structure in vertebrates that contains the organs of balance and hearing. It consists of soft hollow sensory structures (the membranous labyrinth) containing fluid (endolymph) surrounded by fluid (perilymph) and encased in a bony cavity (the bony labyrinth). It consists of two chambers, the sacculus and utriculus, from which arise the cochlea and semicircular canals respectively." [GOC:jl, ISBN:0192801023]
def: "The process in which the anatomical structures of the middle ear are generated and organized. The middle ear is the air-filled cavity within the skull of vertebrates that lies between the outer ear and the inner ear. It is linked to the pharynx (and therefore to outside air) via the Eustachian tube and in mammals contains the three ear ossicles, which transmit auditory vibrations from the outer ear (via the tympanum) to the inner ear (via the oval window)." [GOC:jl, ISBN:0192801023]
def: "The process whose specific outcome is the progression of the nose over time, from its formation to the mature structure. The nose is the specialized structure of the face that serves as the organ of the sense of smell and as part of the respiratory system. Includes the nasi externus (external nose) and cavitas nasi (nasal cavity)." [GOC:jl]
def: "The process in which the anatomical structures of the nose are generated and organized. The nose is the specialized structure of the face that serves as the organ of the sense of smell and as part of the respiratory system. Includes the nasi externus (external nose) and cavitas nasi (nasal cavity)." [GOC:jl]
def: "The process whose specific outcome is the progression of the urinary bladder over time, from its formation to the mature structure. The urinary bladder is an elastic, muscular sac situated in the anterior part of the pelvic cavity in which urine collects before excretion." [GOC:dph, GOC:ln, GOC:mr, PMID:11768524, PMID:18276178, PMID:538956]
def: "The increase in size or mass of a lung. In all air-breathing vertebrates the lungs are developed from the ventral wall of the oesophagus as a pouch which divides into two sacs. In amphibians and many reptiles the lungs retain very nearly this primitive sac-like character, but in the higher forms the connection with the esophagus becomes elongated into the windpipe and the inner walls of the sacs become more and more divided, until, in the mammals, the air spaces become minutely divided into tubes ending in small air cells, in the walls of which the blood circulates in a fine network of capillaries. In mammals the lungs are more or less divided into lobes, and each lung occupies a separate cavity in the thorax." [GOC:dph]
def: "The process in which the anatomical structures of epithelia of the prostate gland are generated and organized. An epithelium consists of closely packed cells arranged in one or more layers, that covers the outer surfaces of the body or lines any internal cavity or tube." [GOC:dph]
def: "The process whose specific outcome is the progression of the anterior Malpighian tubule over time, from its formation to the mature structure. The pair of anterior tubules arise from a dorsal region of the embryonic hindgut and projects forwards through the body cavity. A Malpighian tubule is a fine, thin-walled excretory tubule in insects which connects with the posterior part of the gut." [GOC:dph, GOC:mtg_kidney_jan10, PMID:19783135]
def: "The process whose specific outcome is the progression of the posterior Malpighian tubule over time, from its formation to the mature structure. The pair of posterior tubules arise from a ventrolateral region of the embryonic hindgut and project backwards through the body cavity. A Malpighian tubule is a fine, thin-walled excretory tubule in insects which connects with the posterior part of the gut." [GOC:dph, GOC:mtg_kidney_jan10]
def: "The progression of the olfactory placode over time from its initial formation until its mature state. The olfactory placode is a thickening of the neural ectoderm in the head region of the vertebrate embryo which develops into the olfactory region of the nasal cavity." [GOC:mah]
def: "The process in which the anatomical structures of the olfactory placode are generated and organized. The olfactory placode is a thickening of the neural ectoderm in the head region of the vertebrate embryo which develops into the olfactory region of the nasal cavity." [GOC:mah]

content.content:
[Term]
id: UBERON:0001016
name: nervous system
namespace: uberon
def: "The nervous system is an organ system containing predominantly neuron and glial cells. In bilaterally symmetrical organism, it is arranged in a network of tree-like structures connected to a central body. The main functions of the nervous system are to regulate and control body functions, and to receive sensory input, process this information, and generate behavior [CUMBO]." [BIRNLEX:844, ISBN:0-14-051288-8, ISBN:3110148986, NLM:nervous+system, WB:rynl, Wikipedia:Nervous_system, ZFIN:curator]
subset: cumbo
subset: efo_slim
subset: human_reference_atlas

#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""Check which UBERON terms are NOT subclasses of UBERON:0010000."""

import re
import sys
from collections import defaultdict

def parse_obo(path):
    stanzas = []
    cur = None
    with open(path) as f:
        for line in f:
            line = line.rstrip('\n')
            if line.startswith('[Term]') or line.startswith('[Typedef]'):
                if cur is not None:
                    stanzas.append(cur)
                cur = {'_type': line.strip('[]'), '_lines': []}
            elif line == '' or not line.strip():
                continue
            elif cur is None:
                continue
            else:
                cur['_lines'].append(line)
                if ': ' in line:
                    key, _, val = line.partition(': ')
                    cur.setdefault(key, []).append(val)
    if cur is not None:
        stanzas.append(cur)
    return stanzas

stanzas = parse_obo('/__w/go-ontology/go-ontology/src/ontology/imports/uberon_import.obo')

parents = defaultdict(set)
all_terms = {}
for s in stanzas:
    if s['_type'] != 'Term':
        continue
    if s.get('is_obsolete', ['false'])[0] == 'true':
        continue
    cid = s.get('id', [None])[0]
    if cid is None:
        continue
    all_terms[cid] = s.get('name', ['?'])[0]
    for ia in s.get('is_a', []):
        pid = ia.split(' ')[0]
        parents[cid].add(pid)
    # Also use equivalence axioms via intersection_of? In edit ontology these become reasoned subclass-of. The import is already mireot-extracted; check if reasoning needed.

def ancestors(cid):
    seen = set()
    stack = list(parents.get(cid, set()))
    while stack:
        p = stack.pop()
        if p in seen:
            continue
        seen.add(p)
        stack.extend(parents.get(p, set()))
    return seen

# Check what's under UBERON:0010000 (multicellular anatomical structure)
under_mas = set()
for cid in all_terms:
    if 'UBERON:0010000' in ancestors(cid):
        under_mas.add(cid)
print(f"UBERON terms under UBERON:0010000: {len(under_mas)} / {len(all_terms)} total UBERON terms", file=sys.stderr)

# Check specific terms from the analysis
test_terms = [
    'UBERON:0004122',  # urogenital system
    'UBERON:0000358',  # blastocyst
    'UBERON:0002450',  # decidua
    'UBERON:0002049',  # vasculature
    'UBERON:0000965',  # lens
    'UBERON:0002405',  # immune system
    'UBERON:0001016',  # nervous system
    'UBERON:0001785',  # cranial nerve
    'UBERON:0000010',  # peripheral nervous system
    'UBERON:0000062',  # organ
    'UBERON:0000479',  # tissue
    'UBERON:0001134',  # skeletal muscle tissue
    'UBERON:0002553',  # anatomical cavity
    'UBERON:0000464',  # anatomical space
    'UBERON:0000061',  # anatomical structure
    'UBERON:0000467',  # anatomical system
    'UBERON:0010000',  # multicellular anatomical structure
]
for t in test_terms:
    name = all_terms.get(t, '?')
    a = ancestors(t)
    in_mas = 'UBERON:0010000' in a
    print(f"{t}  {name}  -> under UBERON:0010000? {in_mas}")
    if not in_mas and t != 'UBERON:0010000':
        # Show path
        path_parents = sorted(a)
        # Show just one or two ancestors
        for p in list(a)[:5]:
            print(f"    ancestor: {p}  ({all_terms.get(p, '?')})")

namespace: biological_process

input.content:
@raymond91125 Yes — quite a lot of animal body-part developmental processes are not reasoning under **GO:0160108** as it stands. Your body-cavity example doesn't have a direct match in GO (no `body cavity development`-named term), but it points at the right problem: the current LD is too narrow.

## Headline number

Of the **1040** descendants of `GO:0048856 anatomical structure development`, only **555** classify under `GO:0160108`. **484** terms fall in the gap. Of those, **317 are animal body parts that should be under GO:0160108** (the remaining 167 are correctly excluded: 99 CL cells, 53 PO plants, 10 FAO fungi, 4 GO subcellular, 1 DDANAT).

## Why they don't classify — root cause

GO:0160108's equivalence axiom is:

> `anatomical structure development` ⊓ `results_in_development_of` some `UBERON:0010000` (multicellular anatomical structure)

In the local `uberon_import.obo`, **only 3564 of 4783 UBERON classes are subclasses of `UBERON:0010000`**. Several major UBERON parents that *should* sit under `multicellular anatomical structure` are not classified there in the import — so the GO development terms that point at them are stranded.

## The four big buckets of missing animal terms

### 1. Organ systems — `UBERON:0000467 anatomical system` (11 + descendants)

These are unambiguously animal gross anatomical parts but the parent isn't tagged as multicellular:

| GO ID | Term | UBERON target |
|---|---|---|
| GO:0048731 | system development | UBERON:0000467 anatomical system |
| GO:0007399 | nervous system development | UBERON:0001016 |
| GO:0001655 | urogenital system development | UBERON:0004122 |
| GO:0055123 | digestive system development | UBERON:0001007 |
| GO:0060541 | respiratory system development | UBERON:0001004 |
| GO:0072001 | renal system development | UBERON:0001008 |
| GO:0072359 | circulatory system development | UBERON:0001009 |
| GO:0061458 | reproductive system development | UBERON:0000990 |
| GO:0007424 | open tracheal system development | UBERON:0005155 |
| GO:0035272 | exocrine system development | UBERON:0002330 |
| GO:0021591 | ventricular system development | UBERON:0005281 |
| GO:0030539 | male genitalia development | UBERON:0000079 |

Plus the 7 sibling "system" terms under `UBERON:0015203 non-connected functional system`: GO:0002520 immune system, GO:0035270 endocrine system, GO:0048880 sensory system, GO:0048925 lateral line system, GO:0160038 somatosensory system, GO:0048898 / GO:0048915 anterior/posterior lateral line system.

### 2. Neuroanatomy parts — `UBERON:0005162 multi cell part structure` (59 terms)

Brain nuclei, cortical layers, spinal cord regions, etc. Examples:
- GO:0003407 neural retina development, GO:0021987 cerebellar cortex development
- GO:0021764 amygdala development, GO:0021759 globus pallidus development
- GO:0021756 striatum development, GO:0021757 caudate nucleus development
- GO:0021679 cerebellar molecular layer development, GO:0021680 Purkinje cell layer
- spinal cord, brainstem nuclei (~30 nuclei terms in the GO:0021713–GO:0021771 range)

### 3. Cranial / lateral-line nerves — `UBERON:0001021 nerve` branch (14 terms)

The "nerve" branch sits under `multi cell part structure` but not under MAS:
- GO:0021545 cranial nerve, GO:0021553 olfactory, GO:0021554 optic, GO:0021557 oculomotor, GO:0021558 trochlear, GO:0021559 trigeminal, GO:0021560 abducens, GO:0021561 facial, GO:0021562 vestibulocochlear, GO:0021563 glossopharyngeal, GO:0021564 vagus, GO:0021565 accessory, GO:0021566 hypoglossal, plus GO:0048892/0048909/0048918 lateral line nerves.

### 4. Developing / embryonic structures — `UBERON:0005423 developing anatomical structure` + `UBERON:0000478 extraembryonic structure` (15 terms)

- GO:0001824 blastocyst development, GO:0001893 maternal placenta (decidua), GO:0061027 umbilical cord
- GO:0071696 ectodermal placode, GO:0071698 olfactory placode, GO:1905040 otic placode
- GO:0061054 dermatome, GO:0061055 myotome, GO:0036302 atrioventricular canal
- GO:0070121 Kupffer's vesicle, GO:0072360 vascular cord, GO:0007418 ventral midline, GO:0001748 insect visual primordium, GO:0061079/0061080 sinus venosus horns

### Plus other smaller buckets (~28 terms)
- Vasculature (6: GO:0001944, GO:0060426 lung, GO:0061298 retina, GO:0061300 cerebellum, GO:0061440 kidney, GO:0016319 mushroom body)
- Misc UBERON targets that aren't tagged multicellular: lens, cardiac septa (7 terms), beak, otolith, somatic muscle, deltoid tuberosity, cuticles, juxtaglomerular apparatus, glomerular basement membrane, several gyri/cortex regions

## Plus 175 terms with no LD at all

These have `is_a: GO:0048856` (or descendant) but no `results_in_development_of` axiom, so the reasoner has nothing to bite. Highlights that are clearly animal:
- **GO:0048513 animal organ development** ← explicit "animal" in the name
- GO:0048534 hematopoietic or lymphoid organ development
- GO:0048608 reproductive structure development
- Drosophila: GO:0007477 notum, GO:0007487 analia, GO:0007496/0007497 anterior/posterior midgut, GO:0048098 antennal joint, GO:0007526 larval somatic muscle, GO:0048722–GO:0048728 cibarial plate / clypeus / epistomal sclerite / labrum / proboscis, GO:0048806 genitalia
- C. elegans: GO:0040025 vulval development
- Vertebrate: GO:0007421 stomatogastric nervous system, GO:0036306 embryonic heart tube elongation, GO:0044691 tooth eruption, GO:0072013 glomus, GO:0039012 pronephric sinus
- Lateral line: GO:0048881, GO:0048887 cupula, GO:0048904, GO:0048921, GO:0048926
- Regeneration: GO:0031099–GO:0031104, GO:0042246 tissue, GO:0043403 skeletal muscle, GO:0061026 cardiac muscle, GO:0097719 neural tissue, GO:1990523 bone, GO:1990798 pancreas

## Suggested directions

I see three workable paths, none of which I'd merge unilaterally without your call:

1. **Loosen the genus of GO:0160108** — use `UBERON:0000061 anatomical structure` instead of `UBERON:0010000 multicellular anatomical structure`. Risk: brings in cells (CL is a subclass of UBERON:0000061), defeating the "a cell is not a gross anatomical part" principle. Would need a complementary exclusion.

2. **Fix the UBERON import** — extend the MIREOT slice so the missing parents (`anatomical system`, `multi cell part structure`, `developing anatomical structure`, `nerve`, `anatomical cluster`, `non-connected functional system`, `extraembryonic structure`) carry their full subclass paths to `UBERON:0010000`. This is the cleanest fix and gets ~140 terms automatically. May require coordination with Uberon if those parents aren't classified that way upstream either.

3. **Add the missing logical definitions** — for the 175 terms with no `results_in_development_of` axiom. Most are tractable but each needs an appropriate UBERON target chosen.

Happy to go after (3) in batches, or to draft the import-extension for (2), once you've decided which way you want to take this. Sub-issue or do it in this one?

content.text:
Posted the analysis on issue #22994. Summary of what I found:

- **484** terms are under `GO:0048856 anatomical structure development` but not under `GO:0160108`. Of those, **317** are animal body-part developmental processes that *should* classify.
- Root cause: the LD uses `UBERON:0010000 multicellular anatomical structure`, but in the local uberon import several major animal-anatomy parents (anatomical system, multi-cell part structure, developing anatomical structure, nerve, anatomical cluster, extraembryonic structure, non-connected functional system) aren't subclasses of `UBERON:0010000`, so their development terms are stranded.
- Big buckets identified: 11 organ-system terms, 59 neuroanatomy parts (nuclei, cortical layers, etc.), 14 cranial/lateral-line nerves, 15 developing/embryonic structures, plus ~28 misc UBERON targets and **175 terms with no `results_in_development_of` LD at all** (including `GO:0048513 animal organ development` itself).
- No GO term explicitly named "body cavity development" exists — your example was illustrative.
- Suggested three remediation paths (loosen the genus / extend the UBERON import / add missing LDs) and asked which to pursue. No code changes pushed yet, since the right fix is a curation decision.
